Author: 246tnt
Content: @Tim Edwards BTW, basic testing seem to show that IO library works in silicon 🙂

Author: 246tnt
Content: Changed the channel name: Using gf180mcu_ocd_io in LibreLane

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: @tnt :  What kind of "basic testing"?

Author: 246tnt
Content: Well, they're fed 3.3v on both Vio and Vcore and they pass data between the inside and the outside of the chip 😅

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: What chip was that?  I thought I was the only one using my I/O library.

Author: 246tnt
Content: That's the extend of the testing that will be done at the factory until I get my hands on the chips.

Author: 246tnt
Content: We have a version of the tiny tapeout GF 0.2 that uses your library.

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: Oh, wow, I didn't know that.  Nice!

Author: 246tnt
Content: Hopefully by end of next week I'll be able to make actual delay / rise / fall measurements and IV curves and all that stuff with different Vio and Vcore .

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: I don't think I ever actually tested operation with Vio at 3.3V.

Author: 246tnt
Content: I simulated that case and it's behaving just fine AFAICT. Pretty much same results as the original GF pads at 3.3v.

Author: 246tnt
Content: Some of your dies should be on their way to you so if you have bonding capability locally you can test those too 🙂 Looking fwd to seeing the 3.3V SRAM results 😁

Author: mithro_
Content: @tnt - The first package of Tiny Tapeout bonded die went out to you today.

Content: @Tim 'mithro' Ansell Got a tracking number ? 😅

Author: mithro_

Author: 246tnt

Author: 246tnt

Author: 246tnt
Content: @Tim Edwards Did a bit more testing and they seem to perform just fine at 3.3V    Got rise time of 2 ns and fall time of 1 ns ( not sure of the load, it's a test pcb + scope probe so probably in the 20~30 pF range ). And the output still looked pretty square outputting a 75 MHz clock.   TBH they work better than the sky130 and IHP ones 😅

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: The sky130 pads are over-designed and the IHP pads are under-designed, so that's not entirely surprising.  Are you planning any tests at 5V?

Author: 246tnt
Content: @Tim Edwards Yes I am. But the breakout I have is unsuitable for 5V test ATM, so I need to wait until I get one where I can set Vio to what I want.

Author: 246tnt
Content: BTW there are diodes between Vcore and Vio, so Vio needs to come up first

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: I'm not sure if I looked specifically for diodes between the power supplies.  Would there happen to be a forward-biased diode from Vio to Vcore?  That would be a big problem for running the I/O voltage at 5V.

Author: 246tnt
Content: No, it's forward biased from Vcore to Vio.  So must have Vio >= Vcore

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: In what cell(s)?  A quick check of the netlist for `D` entries shows only diodes between pad and power, and between ground and power.

Author: 246tnt
Content: Ah ... that I haven't searched yet, I can just tell you I see it in the silicon 😅

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: Probably some indirect path like through a pullup resistor or something.

Author: 246tnt
Content: I was feeding 3.3V to Vcore and I haden't power Vio yet and still the IO were powered through it ...

Author: rtimothyedwards_19428
Content: I'll have to go analyze that one.
